title: sterculia robusta
intro: Sterculia robusta, also known as the Malabar chestnut, is a plant species native to tropical Asia, characterized by its large leaves and edible seeds. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Sterculia robusta include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and aid in the treatment of diabetes and obesity. The seeds can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, and are also used in traditional cooking, particularly in Asian cuisine.
